title: from_raw doc: state the caller invariant, not just what's bypassed
description: Id::from_raw and UTCDate::from_raw doc comments say 'bypassing the non-empty validation' and 'Only use for server-assigned identifiers and test fixtures.' This explains *what* is skipped but not *why* that skip is safe. A contributor unfamiliar with the pattern may use from_raw for any string they 'already have,' including ones that are not server-guaranteed. The doc should state the positive invariant the caller must uphold: the caller asserts the string is non-empty because it came from a trusted source (server JSON or a hardcoded test vector). Without that, the warning reads as a usage hint rather than a safety contract.
close_reason: Updated from_raw docs on Id and UTCDate to state caller invariant (not just what's bypassed)
